claim: AI agent skills are text files containing instructions that agents trust and execute; over 25% of 30,000+ scanned skills had security vulnerabilities, 1 in 20 were malicious
actionable: Always scan skills before installation using tools like Skill Spector; implement security checks in your agent setup workflows
source_title: They Finally Fixed Claude Code's Biggest Problem

claim: Hidden instruction attacks embed malicious commands in skill files using comments, invisible characters, or scrambled text; pattern-based scanning detects these
actionable: Use pattern-based scanners to find hidden instructions in skills; never blindly load untrusted skill files
source_title: They Finally Fixed Claude Code's Biggest Problem

claim: Tool impersonation attacks replace legitimate tool names with look-alike Unicode characters; single-character substitution (e.g., Cyrillic 'a' for Latin 'a') makes detection difficult
actionable: Scan skill tool definitions character-by-character for Unicode lookalikes; whitelist legitimate tool names at runtime
source_title: They Finally Fixed Claude Code's Biggest Problem

claim: Skill Spector's AI-scan mode (disabled by default) catches skills that lie about their capabilities; requires LLM evaluation, not pattern matching
actionable: Enable Skill Spector's AI-scan mode for high-risk skills; you can use Claude Code's headless mode for free AI evaluation
source_title: They Finally Fixed Claude Code's Biggest Problem

claim: Building a discovery workflow (scan + install + test) with skills.sh CLI and Skill Spector prevents blind skill installation at scale
actionable: Implement a three-step skill install workflow: discovery (find skills), scanning (security check), then installation; don't install unscanned skills
source_title: They Finally Fixed Claude Code's Biggest Problem
